g.text: Identify the best inverse scaler for a given resolution.
Compares multiple kernels against a specific target resolution to determine which one was likely used for
Path to the source material to analyze. Supports videos, images, or
VapourSynth scripts. For scripts, the first output is used.
The suspected native resolution to verify. Use an integer for exact pixels
(e.g., 720) or a float for sub-pixel dimensions (e.g., 719.8).
Base integer dimension if checking for fractional resolution.
Edge-detection mask to reduce noise influence on the metric. Pass a mask
name (e.g., 'Prewitt') or a class name from vsmasktools.
Specifies whether to analyze based on the
The kernel(s) to use for inverse scaling.
Can be a kernel name or a class call with
The specific frame number to extract and
analyze from video inputs. Ignored for images.
Crop the input frame before analysis to remove
The mathematical metric used to compare
The VapourSynth indexer used to load files.
Specifying the plugin namespace is also
- getscaler gives heuristic results; it's not infallible.
- Always visually verify the suggested scaler and parameters on multiple frames before trusting them.
